headline: Malaysia's anti-money laundering regime has 12 identified gaps says FATF
context: The Financial Action Task Force's 2025 mutual evaluation identified 12 significant gaps in Malaysia's anti-money laundering framework, risking potential greylisting.

cards.big: FATF found 12 major gaps in Malaysia's anti-money laundering system
cards.sub: Greylisting risk threatens Malaysia's access to international financial systems

cards.big: AMLA 2001 has not been comprehensively amended since 2014 despite evolving threats
cards.sub: Legislative framework is a decade behind the money laundering techniques it targets

cards.big: Greylisting could cost Malaysia estimated RM28 billion in reduced foreign investment
cards.sub: Financial reputation damage has direct economic consequences for the entire country

cards.big: Beneficial ownership registry mandated in 2020 covers only 67% of corporate entities
cards.sub: 33% of companies still hide their true owners behind nominee structures

cards.big: 12 gaps in anti-money laundering aren't just technical deficiencies — they're 12 open doors for dirty money
Should Malaysia treat FATF compliance as a national security priority with cabinet-level oversight?
